description_long: This clock tower features a tall, slender structure with a conical, shingled roof topped by a pointed spire. The main body is finished in a warm orange hue, accented by prominent gray stone-like trim around the base, corners, and arched window and door frames. Decorative columns and pilasters add architectural interest to the corners, while circular windows with a radial grille pattern are set into three sides of the tower. One side features an arched doorway, suggesting an entrance, while the other sides are adorned with matching arched recesses. The overall design is reminiscent of late 19th-century European civic architecture, blending ornamental and functional elements. The structure appears robust and is likely constructed from masonry with stone or concrete detailing.
description: A tall, orange and gray clock tower with a conical shingled roof, arched windows, and decorative stone trim, evoking classic European civic architecture.
description_view_0: Front view showing a circular window with a radial grille and prominent stone trim, no visible entrance.
description_view_1: Side view featuring an arched doorway with a circular window above, framed by decorative stonework.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with a circular window and no entrance, identical to view 0.
description_view_3: Side view identical to view 0 and 2, with a circular window and no entrance.
